summarySeed.oneLiner: This page compares AI video generators with native generation capabilities — text-to-video, image-to-video, and avatar-based creation. Tools are grouped by primary workflow: cinematic scene generation, avatar-driven presentations, or short-form social effects. Traditional editors that only use AI for peripheral tasks like auto-captions are excluded.
boundaries: Native AI generation required: text-to-video, image-to-video, or avatar creation.
Excludes editors that only add AI-powered captions or filters without generating the core video.
Compared across pricing, credit limits, watermark policies, export quality, and commercial rights.

howToChooseSeeds.title: Match the tool to your core use case.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: Cinematic B-roll and scene generation (Sora, Runway) require a fundamentally different tool than talking-head avatars (Synthesia, HeyGen) or effects-driven social clips (Pika). Start by narrowing which workflow you actually need.

howToChooseSeeds.title: Verify how many minutes your credits actually buy.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: Most platforms use a credit system. A "$20/mo" plan may only generate 15–25 seconds of video per credit spend. Check the actual minutes-per-month on the tier you plan to buy, not just the headline price.

howToChooseSeeds.title: Confirm commercial licensing before publishing.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: Free tiers on several tools — including Pika and Sora — either restrict commercial use or add watermarks to outputs. If you are publishing for a client or brand, ensure your plan explicitly grants commercial rights.

howToChooseSeeds.title: Check watermark and resolution requirements upfront.
howToChooseSeeds.desc: Watermark removal and 1080p+ exports often require upgrading to a mid-tier or higher plan. Synthesia and HeyGen both remove watermarks starting at $29/mo; Sora's free outputs include watermarks.

groups.title: Cinematic generation
groups.rule: These tools produce photorealistic or visually controlled scene-level video from text or image prompts. They are built for creators who need cinematic B-roll, product shots, or high-fidelity short clips — not template-based editing.

groups.title: Avatar workflows
groups.rule: Platforms in this group center on AI presenter avatars and talking-head output. They suit corporate training, multilingual presentations, and any workflow where a human-like speaker is the primary content format.

groups.title: Short-form effects
groups.rule: This group covers generators optimized for fast creative effects and social-first clips — the kind of content made for TikTok, Reels, and rapid iteration rather than polished long-form video.

tools.name: Sora
tools.verdictSeed: Photorealistic text-to-video generation. Produces 15-second clips (up to 25s in Pro storyboard mode) with strong audio-visual quality.
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Commercial use typically requires a paid subscription

tools.name: Synthesia
tools.verdictSeed: Avatar-based video for corporate training and multilingual presentations. Uses a seat-based model with predictable per-minute costs.
tools.policySeed: Watermark removal is included in the Starter plan ($29/mo) and higher
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Starter plan capped at 10 minutes/month; SCORM export requires Enterprise

tools.name: Pika
tools.verdictSeed: Ultra-fast effects-driven content for TikTok and Reels via the Model 2.2 engine.

tools.name: HeyGen
tools.verdictSeed: Flexible avatar creation with 175+ language support and voice cloning. Consumption-based model with credit add-ons.
tools.mainLimitationSeed: Free plan limited to 3 videos/month at 720p; Creator plan caps Avatar IV to ~10 min/mo

tools.name: Runway
tools.verdictSeed: Creative control for effects-driven and cinematic short clips. Supports professional controls including color grading.
tools.bestForSeed: Creative effects and short cinematic clips

faqSeeds.question: What qualifies a tool for Best AI Video Generators (2026)?
faqSeeds.answerSeed: Group by primary generation workflow. Must have native AI video generation (text-to-video, image-to-video, or avatar generation). Excludes traditional video editors that only use AI for peripheral tasks (like auto-captions) without generating the core video.

faqSeeds.question: What should readers compare first on this page?
faqSeeds.answerSeed: Evaluate Your Core Use Case: Are you creating cinematic B-roll (Sora, Veo, Runway), talking-head presentations (Synthesia, HeyGen), or viral social media clips (Pika, Luma)? Check Cost and Credit Limits: Most platforms use a credit system. Verify how many seconds or minutes of video you actually get per month on standard paid tiers.

faqSeeds.question: Which tools already have internal review coverage?
faqSeeds.answerSeed: Current internal review coverage in this normalized dataset: sora, synthesia, pika, heygen, runway.

reviewNotes: Duplicate raw tool mentions merged: synthesia
